name: Sacred Knowledge
shortDescription: In Babylonian mythology, sacred knowledge encompassed divine secrets and esoteric wisdom crucial for understanding the cosmos, divining the future, and maintaining cosmic order. It was often associated with specific deities and revealed through rituals and omens.
description: Sacred knowledge in Babylonian mythology was a collection of divinely revealed truths and practices considered essential for both the gods and humanity. This knowledge was not readily accessible but was guarded by deities like Ea (Enki), the god of wisdom and magic, and transmitted through select priests, scholars, and diviners. It included understanding the movements of celestial bodies (astrology), interpreting omens (extispicy and other forms of divination), performing correct rituals and incantations, and knowing the names and functions of various deities and spirits. The possession of this knowledge granted power and influence, allowing individuals to predict the future, influence events, and maintain harmony between the human and divine realms. Marduk, as the chief god after defeating Tiamat, was also associated with sacred knowledge, inheriting and further developing the wisdom of Ea. Shamash, the sun god, was considered a source of justice and truth, and his observation of all things made him a conduit for divine knowledge. The pursuit and correct application of sacred knowledge were fundamental to Babylonian religious and intellectual life, underpinning their understanding of the universe and their place within it. Babylonian divination practices, such as extispicy (examining animal entrails), were specifically designed to uncover hidden divine knowledge. Failure to correctly interpret or apply this knowledge could have dire consequences for individuals, communities, and even the cosmos itself.

sources.title: The Ancient Near East: An Anthology of Texts and Pictures
sources.author: James B. Pritchard (ed.)

sources.title: Gods, Demons, and Symbols of Ancient Mesopotamia
sources.author: Jeremy Black and Anthony Green
